requirements.description: Proposals must focus on next-generation AI agents defined as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilize tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.

Proposals must address one or more key AI agent design aspects: robust planning, reasoning and search mechanisms enabling agents to decompose complex tasks into structured subgoals; effective memory and state management integrating short-term and external long-term memory; or integration of external tools and APIs to overcome large AI model limitations.

Proposals should address at least one of the identified research areas: (1) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization; (2) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (3) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agents including mixed AI architectures; (4) advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities for real-world applications.
All proposals must incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.

Proposals must target real-world applications in the Apply AI sectors. Portfolio coverage procedure requires at least one funded project focusing on each of: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving (subject to passing all evaluation thresholds).
All proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with ADRA (European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics) and with the CSA GenAI4EU central Hub (HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18).
Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant International, European, national, or regional initiatives.
